origin.name: United Arab Emirates
origin.death_registration: Local health authority after police NOC
origin.investigating_authority: UAE Police (Dubai Police, Abu Dhabi Police, Sharjah Police)
origin.post_mortem_trigger: Police investigation order; all deaths must be reported to police first
origin.police_noc_note: Every death in the UAE must be reported to police before any other step. The police issue a No Objection Certificate (NOC) which is required before documentation or preparation can begin. Natural deaths: 24-48 hours. Investigation cases: much longer.
origin.mofa_note: UAE death certificate requires MOFA attestation for international validity. Processing: 1-3 days in normal conditions. Longer during Ramadan and Eid.
origin.cremation_note: Non-Muslim cremation available at designated facilities. Requires police NOC and MOFA attestation.
origin.summer_cargo_embargo: Summer temperature embargoes (approximately June to September) may delay airline cargo acceptance. Plan accordingly.
origin.key_document: UAE death certificate with MOFA attestation and Police NOC
origin.source: FCDO UAE travel advice, updated 2025; British Embassy Abu Dhabi guidance

destinations.name: United Kingdom
key_documents: UAE death certificate with MOFA attestation

airlines.name: Emirates SkyCargo
airlines.routes: DXB to LHR, LGW, MAN, BHX (multiple daily)

special_rules: Police must be notified before any other step. This is UAE law, not a formality.
NOC issued within 24-48 hours for natural deaths. Investigation cases take weeks or months.
MOFA processing slower during Ramadan and Eid public holidays.
Summer cargo temperature embargoes approximately June to September may delay flights.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. All UAE documentation must be in certified English. The coroner for the district is notified. Straightforward cases proceed directly to funeral arrangements.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. The British Embassy in Abu Dhabi and Consulate General in Dubai can register the death and advise on documentation. They cannot pay for or arrange repatriation.
destinations.seo_title: UAE to UK Repatriation: Guidance for British Families
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in the UAE. Repatriation to the UK typically takes 7-21 days. Police NOC, MOFA attestation, and cargo flights from Dubai all handled. Contact us 24/7.

destinations.name: Ireland
key_documents: UAE death certificate with MOFA attestation
special_rules: Police NOC required before any other step. Identical to UK repatriation process.
Irish Embassy in Abu Dhabi is the consular contact.
Summer cargo embargoes apply identically to Irish repatriations.
destinations.dest_reception: The Irish funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. All UAE documentation must be in certified English. The Coroner for the district is notified. Straightforward cases proceed to funeral arrangements without delay.
destinations.dest_consular_note: Department of Foreign Affairs emergency line: +353 1 408 2000 (24 hours). The Irish Embassy in Abu Dhabi can register the death and advise on documentation. They cannot pay for or arrange repatriation.
destinations.seo_title: UAE to Ireland: Funeral Repatriation Guidance
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in the UAE and needs to come home to Ireland. Repatriation takes 7-21 days. Police NOC, MOFA attestation, and all documentation handled. Contact us.
